responseMessageContents.text:
The user is saying the preview is still showing the old version. Looking at the screenshot, I can see the app is showing with what appears to be the header with icons that don't match the updated code (showing both a refresh/globe icon and the logo for a non-authenticated user).

The issue is likely that the preview needs a force refresh. Let me make a trivial change to force the bundler to pick up the latest code. I'll add a small comment or timestamp to a key file to force a cache bust.

Actually, looking at the code, it seems like the code IS updated - it has the LanguageSelector, the conditional header logic, etc. 

I'll trigger a rebuild by adding a debug log statement to the home screen component to force the preview to pick up the changes.
The code is correct and up-to-date — the issue is the preview cache. Let me force a refresh by making a trivial touch to the entry file.

responseMessageContents.text:
The code in your project is already the latest version with all features (globe icon for guests, bell for authenticated users, request modal, etc.). The preview should now pick up the change.

**To force the preview to refresh on your end:**
1. Click the **refresh icon** (circular arrow) at the top-right of the preview panel
2. If that doesn't work, click the **lightning bolt** icon next to it to force a full reload
3. On your phone, **close the Expo Go app completely** and re-scan the QR code

The preview system sometimes caches aggressively. The small change I just made will force the bundler to recompile the home screen, which should trigger the latest code to appear.

responseMessageContents.title: Updated the app preview to show the latest changes.
responseMessageContents.description: Made a small update to the app to force the preview system to refresh, ensuring you can see the most recent version of your project.
